title: [Feature]: Example backlog item title
problem: Describe the operational, developer, security, reliability, or documentation problem in enough detail for a maintainer to understand why the work matters.
proposal: Describe the behavior, documentation, command, config field, sink capability, or operator workflow that should exist when this item is complete.
users: Describe who benefits from the work. Do not include private infrastructure, credentials, server addresses, sensitive subjects, or payload examples.
delivery_semantics: Explain whether the change touches commit-then-acknowledge behavior, idempotency, DLQ behavior, retry behavior, or safe redelivery. If it does not, say so explicitly.
security: Identify secrets, payloads, metadata, logging, authentication, filesystem, database, observability, or network concerns. State what must not be logged, exported, or accepted from untrusted input.
acceptance: Unit tests cover relevant happy and unhappy paths.
CHANGELOG.md is updated when user-visible behavior changes.
tests: List exact checks expected before close-out, for example: pytest tests/unit/test_example.py, python scripts/check-markdown-links.py, mkdocs build --strict.
documentation: List the README, docs pages, examples, release notes, or operator guides that should change.
closeout: When this issue closes, include the implementing PR or commit, shipped behavior, tests/checks run, docs and changelog updates, limitations, and follow-up issues.
